title: Union-closed sets conjecture: sharpness of the 1/2 constant
informal_statement: The union-closed sets conjecture (Frankl's conjecture) asserts the constant 1/2: for every finite union-closed family A other than {empty set}, some element belongs to at least half of the sets in A. This packet proves the constant 1/2 is SHARP (cannot be improved): for any real c > 1/2, there is a union-closed family A (other than {empty set}) for which no element reaches a c-fraction of the sets. Witness: A = the powerset of the whole ambient type n; A is union-closed and not {empty set}, and every element i of n belongs to exactly half (2^(|n|-1) out of 2^|n|) of the sets in A, so no element's fraction can exceed 1/2 < c.
informal_proof_idea: By contradiction: assume every union-closed A != {empty set} has some element reaching fraction c > 1/2. First dispose of the case where n is empty (only A = empty set exists, contradicting A != {empty set} vacuously through the existential). For nonempty n, apply the assumption to A = Finset.univ (all subsets of n), which is union-closed and has cardinality 2^|n| > 1, hence != {empty set}. Get a witness element i with c * 2^|n| <= |{x : i in x}|. Compute |{x : i in x}| exactly: the map x -> insert i x is a bijection from subsets of n \ {i} onto {x : i in x} (injective since erase i recovers the original set; surjective since any x containing i equals insert i (x.erase i)), so |{x : i in x}| = 2^(|n|-1). Substituting, c * 2^|n| <= 2^(|n|-1) = 2^|n| / 2, i.e. c <= 1/2, contradicting c > 1/2.
formal_statement_pp: theorem union_closed_sharpness : ∀ {n : Type*} [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n] (c : ℝ), 1 / 2 < c → ¬ (∀ A : Finset (Finset n), A ≠ ({∅} : Finset (Finset n)) → (∀ X ∈ A, ∀ Y ∈ A, X ∪ Y ∈ A) → ∃ i : n, c * (Finset.card A : ℝ) ≤ ((Finset.filter (fun x => i ∈ x) A).card : ℝ))

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: ANTI-OVERCLAIM: this packet proves only that the constant 1/2 in the union-closed sets conjecture is sharp (cannot be replaced by any larger constant), not the conjecture itself, which remains open in general (the upstream file's own `union_closed` theorem for the general case is `sorry`/`research open`, and this corpus's best transported general bound is Yu 2023's ~0.38234 constant via a different, still-open-in-general theorem). Third and largest of `UnionClosed.lean`'s three proof-complete theorems transported this cycle/session (companions: `union_closed_singleton_mem.v1`, `union_closed_univ_card_two.v1` -- the latter authored independently by a concurrent agent in this same cycle). Restated (not verbatim) in the same two respects as its companions: the upstream `IsUnionClosed` abbrev is inlined directly as `∀ X ∈ A, ∀ Y ∈ A, X ∪ Y ∈ A`, and the upstream `#`/set-builder-filter notation (confirmed `scoped` behind `open Finset` by grepping the vendored Mathlib source) is spelled out as plain `Finset.card`/`Finset.filter` applications, plus the file-local helper `isUnionClosed_univ n` (used to show `Finset.univ` is union-closed) is inlined as a one-line anonymous proof `(by intro X _ Y _; exact Finset.mem_univ _)` at its single call site rather than transported as a separate helper theorem. Otherwise the proof term is essentially verbatim (only variable renames to avoid ambiguous repeated `this`/anonymous `have`s during manual transcription). Kernel-verified on the FIRST attempt (episode d3e6c1ee-ddc3-49e6-b774-6e63e9f8db8e) using `proof_format: raw_lean_block` to preserve the upstream proof's bullet structure exactly (two top-level bullets for the `isEmpty_or_nonempty` case split, plus a nested `constructor`/two-bullet split and a final unbulleted-continuation pattern inside the `hcardi` sub-proof, all copied verbatim). `training.eligibility: quarantined` per this session's default policy for adapted/restated frontier packets; `open_problem_related: true` since the underlying conjecture remains open in general.

idea_attributions.notes: Proof term transported/adapted from this file per the packet's own authoring notes (manual transcription, essentially verbatim proof structure).
Cited as general background for the conjecture statement; not the direct proof source (that is the FormalConjectures Lean file, see the other attribution).

prior_art_matches.notes: This packet proves only that the 1/2 constant is SHARP (cannot be improved) for the family of counterexamples it constructs — a narrow, different claim from Yu 2023's improved general-case bound (~0.38234) for the conjecture itself, which remains open in general. Not a duplicate result; flagged here so a reviewer can confirm the two are not conflated. Confidence 0.5 reflects that the Yu 2023 bibliographic details are themselves unverified (see literature_sources/ catalog entry).

citation_reviews.notes: Attribution to the FormalConjectures source and Wikipedia background is well-documented in the packet's pre-existing notes/source_provenance. The Yu 2023 prior-art flag is retained at lower confidence pending an independent bibliography check.
